rounds.tips: Discussing about the project works for most of the time and the technical questions were asked at the last 15 minutes. Prepare accordingly.

technical_questions.question: Given an expression string s, examine whether the pairs and orders of {, }, (, ), [, ] are correct.
technical_questions.solution: Using stack to ensure every opening has a matching closing.

technical_questions.question: Given an array of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals and return the array having only non-overlapping intervals.
technical_questions.solution: Merge overlapping intervals after sorting and comparing.

rounds.tips: Discussing about the project works for most of the time and the technical questions were asked at the last 15 minutes. Prepare accordingly.

key_tips: Know every detail of the projects mentioned in the resume.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't hesitate to ask doubts that are valid.

technical_questions.question: Given an array, output the cumulative sum (indirectly specified in the problem).
technical_questions.solution: Input: [1,2,5,6] → Output: 26 (sum of all prefix sums)

technical_questions.question: Based on the Prefix and Suffix Max Array.
technical_questions.solution: Build prefix and suffix arrays and simultaneously traverse both.

rounds.tips: No behavioral questions. Only self-intro is asked.

technical_questions.question: Given energy levels, decay rates, and a minimum threshold for magical stones, return the maximum time the shield will survive.
Given a grid with unblocked paths, blocked paths, and traps, return the shortest path to the destination.
technical_questions.solution: Slight variation of Dijkstra's algorithm to accommodate traps.

key_tips: Focus on explaining the solution instead of directly coding. Make sure the interviewer understands your approach before you start coding.
mistakes_to_avoid: Try to be open-minded with the interviewer and explain all the approaches you thought for the particular problem.

technical_questions.question: Find triplets satisfying a custom condition

rounds.tips: Explain approach on whiteboard; think aloud; write code on paper without IDE; consider edge cases; be strong in one language and know its internals.

technical_questions.question: Delete a node with given value in a singly linked list; follow-up on memory management and memory leaks.
Given a circular linked list, delete every kth node until a single node remains.
Lifecycle of objects in heap.
technical_questions.solution: Discussed young generation (eden, survivor spaces) and old generation, and types of GC.

technical_questions.question: Multithreading in Java.
technical_questions.solution: Threads via Runnable/Thread class, race condition, synchronized, notify/wait, Semaphore, ExecutorService, CompletableFuture.

rounds.tips: Be ready for everything; stay calm; do real-world projects; give possible related answers.

technical_questions.question: Group Anagrams - standard DSA question; focus on edge cases.
System Design: Design a video streaming platform for students and professors (professors upload, students stream in parallel) in a cost-effective way.
technical_questions.solution: Client, API gateway with rate limiting, microservice architecture, block storage, caching, CDN, metadata storage, Docker, Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana.

technical_questions.question: Open a project repo from GitHub (Real-Time Monitoring System) and explain every feature and discuss extensibility for new features.
Computer Networks: TCP, UDP, Routing, DNS, what happens when you type google.com.
technical_questions.solution: Relate everything with practical application and real-time usage of different protocols.

rounds.tips: Don't tell them your higher studies plan. Give polished answers.

technical_questions.question: Brief introduction, relocation, experience, higher studies plan.

mistakes_to_avoid: Having superficial understanding of something and having that on the resume.
